sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 67, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: LEFT selected the left box (c→G) at [28-31, 16-19]. The former white box reverted to c. Player at [28-31, 21-23]. Left box is now G (selected).
- Plan: Press SPACE to activate the left box (G→W), completing the level.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 66 of about 71 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The left box activated! It changed from G to W at [28-31, 16-20] (expanded to 4x5, px=16). But the level is not completed yet.
Current state:
- Left box: W at [28-31, 16-20] (activated)
- Player: N at [28-31, 21-23]
- Former white box: c at [32-35, 20-23] (reverted to inactive)
- Created box: c at [32-35, 36-39] (still inactive)
So now I have:
1. Left box: W (activated)
2. Former white box: c (inactive, needs activation)
3. Created box: c (inactive, needs activation)
Wait, but earlier I activated 3 boxes total. Let me recount:
1. First box (originally at right): activated to W
2. Second box (created by SPACE): activated to W
3. Third box (another created one): activated to W
4. Left box: just activated to W
But the former white box reverted to c. So now I need to activate it again, plus the created box at [32-35, 36-39].
Actually, let me reconsider. The pattern seems to be:
- When you activate a box (G→W), it becomes the "white box" that follows the player
- When you activate another box, the previous white box reverts to c
- The goal is to activate ALL boxes
So now I need to:
1. Activate the c box at [32-35, 20-23] (former white box)
2. Activate the c box at [32-35, 36-39] (created box)
But wait, activating a box makes it the new white box, which reverts the previous one. So I can't have all boxes as W simultaneously. Unless... the goal is to activate them in a specific order or pattern.
Let me look at the current state more carefully:
- Left box: W at [28-31, 16-20]
- Player: N at [28-31, 21-23]
- Box below: c at [32-35, 20-23]
- Box right: c at [32-35, 36-39]
The player is at [28-31, 21-23], which is to the RIGHT of the left box (W). The box below is at [32-35, 20-23], which is BELOW the player. The box right is at [32-35, 36-39], which is to the RIGHT and BELOW.
To activate the box below at [32-35, 20-23], I need to:
1. Move toward it to select it (c→G)
2. Press SPACE to activate it (G→W)
The player is at [28-31, 21-23]. The box below is at [32-35, 20-23]. The player is ABOVE and slightly to the RIGHT of the box below.
If I move DOWN, the player would go to [32-34, 21-23] (or similar), which overlaps with the box below at [32-35, 20-23]. That might be blocked.
If I move LEFT, the player would go to [28-31, 17-19] (or similar), which overlaps with the left box at [28-31, 16-20]. That might be blocked.
If I move RIGHT, the player would go to [28-31, 24-26] (or similar).
If I move UP, the player would go to [25-27, 21-23] (or similar).
Let me try DOWN first to see if I can select the box below.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Left box activated (W) at [28-31, 16-20]. Former white box reverted to c at [32-35, 20-23]. Created box still c at [32-35, 36-39]. Player at [28-31, 21-23]. Need to activate remaining c boxes.
Plan: Try DOWN to select the box below at [32-35, 20-23], then SPACE to activate.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at 28-31, 21-23
# Box below at 32-35, 20-23 (c)
# Box right at 32-35, 36-39 (c)
# Try DOWN to select box below

result = action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After DOWN:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'W', 'N'] and n['pixels'] >= 8:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

  color=N, rows [28-31], cols [21-23], px=12
  color=c, rows [32-35], cols [20-23], px=12
  color=c, rows [32-35], cols [36-39], px=12
  color=c, rows [63-63], cols [43-63], px=21
